title: Conjugation of Thalassiosira pseudonana
author: Jernej Turnsek
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.f55bq86

steps.title: Pick bacterial colonies from your Gent+Kan...
steps.instruction: Pick bacterial colonies from your Gent+Kan plates and inoculate 10 mL LB medium. Grow overnight. Start growing colonies early in the afternoon the day before conjugation to get enough biomass. ~16-20 hrs should suffice. Expect OD 600 ~2 next morning.

steps.title: Measure OD 600 and start a...
steps.instruction: Measure OD 600 and start a 150 mL LB subculture (recommended starting OD 600 either 0.05 or 0.1).

steps.title: Grow at 37 o C until OD 600 reaches 0
steps.instruction: Grow at 37 o C until OD 600 reaches 0.3-0.4.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4,000 rpm, 10 o C, for 10 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4,000 rpm, 10 o C, for 10 min. 00:10:00

steps.title: Decant supernatant and resuspend in 800 µL SOC
steps.instruction: Decant supernatant and resuspend in 800 µL SOC.

steps.title: Tp was cultured in L1 medium...
steps.instruction: Tp was cultured in L1 medium as described in the 'Before start' section. - I've never tested if cell density before spinning cells down matters with respect to final conjugation outcome same as the Alverson Lab did for P. tricornutum. They observe ~8 x 10 6 cells/mL to be a sweet spot. In my hands spinning cells down at ~4-8 x 10 6 cells/mL seemed to work fine. According to my information harvesting T. pseudonana at ~0.8 x 10 5 cell/mL works best for biolistic experiments. - I counted cells with BioRad's TC20 automatic cell counter .

steps.title: Spin down 2 x 10 8...
steps.instruction: Spin down 2 x 10 8 cells at 4000 rpm, 10 o C, for 10 min. 00:10:00

steps.title: Decant supernatant and resuspend pellet in 1 mL L1 medium
steps.instruction: Decant supernatant and resuspend pellet in 1 mL L1 medium. I usually saved some supernatant and used it for resuspension and later on for scraping cells before selection (see Steps 14 and 15).

steps.title: Mix 200 µL T
steps.instruction: Mix 200 µL T. pseudonana cells and 200 µL E. coli cells in a 1.5 mL tube.

steps.title: Pipette up and down a few times
steps.instruction: Pipette up and down a few times.

steps.title: Plate on 1/2xL1 1% agar plates w/ 5% LB
steps.instruction: Plate on 1/2xL1 1% agar plates w/ 5% LB. Make sure the plates are dry. Leave them open at 37 0 C for an hour or so before plating diatom-bacteria co-cultures.

steps.title: Incubate in dark at 30 o C for 90 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate in dark at 30 o C for 90 minutes. 01:30:00

steps.title: Move plates to your standard Tp...
steps.instruction: Move plates to your standard Tp growth conditions - in my case 18 o C and constant light - for 4 hours. 04:00:00

steps.title: Add 1 mL L1 medium and...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL L1 medium and scrape with a cell scraper or L spreader .

steps.title: Expect to recover ~500 µL co-culture suspension after scraping
steps.instruction: Expect to recover ~500 µL co-culture suspension after scraping.

steps.title: Plate 200 µL of the resulting...
steps.instruction: Plate 200 µL of the resulting suspension on pre-dried 1/2xL1 1% agar plates w/ 100 µg/mL nourseothricin.

steps.title: Leave at 18 o C and...
steps.instruction: Leave at 18 o C and constant light until colonies appear - ~2 weeks.

steps.title: Here are a few ways to...
steps.instruction: Here are a few ways to confirm the presence and expression of your heterologous gene in resulting colonies: 1. Growth under selection pressure. Make sure colonies are able to grow in liquid L1 medium with 100 µg/mL nourseothricin (Nou100). Pick colonies with a small tip or better a toothpick and inoculate ~0.5 mL L1 medium. Once you observe growth subculture in larger volume. 2. PCR - Use 1 µL of diatom culture as a template to amplify your expression cassette. - Confirm the absence of donor DNA by amplifying E. coli -specific genes. NOTE: I used primers to amplify the corC gene. Make sure you always run E. coli EPI300 posititve control. - Confirm the absence of live donor cells by plating some diatom culture on LB plates w/o antibiotics. NOTE: Any remaining donors cells and donor DNA are gone after a few liquid subcultures. You can also patch colonies on fresh 1/2xL1 Nou100 plates. 3. RT-PCR - Purify total RNA from Nou100 diatom culture, convert it to cDNA and use it to run a PCR with heterologous gene-specific primers. - An example of my result with controls can be found here . 4. Episome recovery - Perform a diatom miniprep as described by Karas et al. (2015). - Transform E. coli with diatom-derived DNA. - Select on LB agar plates with 50 µg/mL kanamycin. - Miniprep, digest and analyze on a gel. NOTE: You will learn more about the state of your episome from this analysis. 5. Western blot 6. Protein-specific assays - enzymatic assay, microscopy etc. I've had success with 1, 2 and 3; tried 4, 5 and 6 without success.
